title: Orange Shadows at Sundown
synopsis: When Waddles the baby emperor penguin gets separated from his class field trip to the pumpkin patch, he must navigate the golden hour maze of vines and shadows alone, discovering unexpected friendships with the patch's unusual residents along the way.

pages.text: The school bus coughs three times and drives away without me. I watch it get smaller through the pumpkin vines, my flippers pressed against my round belly. Mrs. Featherworth's voice fades with the engine noise, still counting heads that don't include mine. The pumpkin patch stretches in every direction, orange globes sitting like dropped suns in the afternoon light. I am standing next to a pumpkin twice my height, and it occurs to me that being lost might be the most interesting thing that's happened all week.

illustration.description: A chubby baby emperor penguin with fluffy grey-white down, round shiny black eyes, a tiny yellow beak, and oversized webbed feet stands dwarfed by an enormous orange pumpkin, watching a small yellow school bus disappear down a dirt road in the distance, with golden afternoon light streaming across rows of pumpkins
illustration.alt: Waddles watching the school bus leave him behind in the pumpkin patch

pages.text: My oversized webbed feet make squelching sounds in the soft earth between the rows. I try to remember which way the class went, but every path looks exactly the same—just more orange spheres and twisting green vines that grab at my fluffy down. A crow perched on a nearby pumpkin tilts her head at me. 'You're supposed to follow the group,' she says matter-of-factly. I shrug, which is harder to do when you have flippers instead of shoulders. 'Groups are overrated,' I tell her, though my voice squeaks a little on the word 'overrated.'

illustration.description: A chubby baby emperor penguin with fluffy grey-white down, round shiny black eyes, a tiny yellow beak, and oversized webbed feet stands among twisting pumpkin vines, looking up at a sleek black crow perched on a bright orange pumpkin, with dappled sunlight filtering through the patch
illustration.alt: Waddles talking to a crow in the pumpkin maze

pages.text: I discover that pumpkins have opinions about everything. The medium-sized ones think they're too good for the small ones, and the giant ones pretend the medium ones don't exist. I'm waddling between two particularly snooty specimens when a voice pipes up from near my feet. 'Down here, penguin!' A tiny pumpkin, no bigger than my head, waves a curly vine at me. 'I'm Pip,' she says. 'And I think you're going the wrong way.' I look around at the maze of orange. Every direction looks wrong, which is oddly comforting.

illustration.description: A chubby baby emperor penguin with fluffy grey-white down, round shiny black eyes, a tiny yellow beak, and oversized webbed feet bends down to look at a tiny orange pumpkin with a cheerful face, surrounded by much larger pumpkins that seem to be ignoring them both, with late afternoon shadows growing longer
illustration.alt: Waddles meeting Pip the tiny pumpkin

pages.text: 'The exit is that way,' Pip points with her vine toward what looks like more pumpkins to me. 'But the interesting stuff is this way,' she points in the opposite direction. I consider this. Mrs. Featherworth always says to take the safe path, but Mrs. Featherworth also counted wrong and left me here. 'Interesting stuff it is,' I decide. Pip grins, which is impressive for a pumpkin. 'I was hoping you'd say that. Nobody ever chooses interesting over easy.' My webbed feet start squelching toward whatever interesting means in a place like this.

illustration.description: A chubby baby emperor penguin with fluffy grey-white down, round shiny black eyes, a tiny yellow beak, and oversized webbed feet follows a tiny grinning pumpkin with a curly vine arm along a winding dirt path, with warm golden light casting playful shadows through the patch
illustration.alt: Waddles following Pip deeper into the pumpkin patch

pages.text: Pip leads me to a section where the pumpkins grow in perfect circles, like someone planned a very orange meeting. 'This is where we hold the evening discussions,' she explains. 'About what?' I ask, settling my round body between two medium pumpkins who scoot over to make room. 'About the humans who visit,' says a deep voice from a particularly large pumpkin. 'We take notes.' This strikes me as hilarious, since I've never seen a pumpkin with a pencil, but I don't mention it. Everyone deserves their hobbies.

illustration.description: A chubby baby emperor penguin with fluffy grey-white down, round shiny black eyes, a tiny yellow beak, and oversized webbed feet sits in a circle of pumpkins of various sizes, all with animated faces, as golden evening light creates a warm glow around their meeting, with tiny Pip gesturing importantly
illustration.alt: Waddles in a circle of talking pumpkins during their evening meeting

pages.text: The pumpkins have been watching humans all season, and their observations are surprisingly astute. 'They always pick the roundest ones,' notes a medium pumpkin. 'But roundness doesn't equal happiness,' argues another. 'Some of us are happier lumpy.' I find myself nodding along, which feels natural even though I'm discussing philosophy with vegetables. The sun is getting lower, painting everything the color of butter and honey. For the first time since the bus left, I'm not thinking about being lost.

illustration.description: A chubby baby emperor penguin with fluffy grey-white down, round shiny black eyes, a tiny yellow beak, and oversized webbed feet nods thoughtfully while listening to animated pumpkins of different shapes and sizes sharing their wisdom, with warm golden sunset light washing over the entire scene
illustration.alt: Waddles listening to pumpkin philosophy at sunset

pages.text: The golden hour is almost over when I hear Mrs. Featherworth calling my name. Her voice cuts through the evening air like a very worried foghorn. The pumpkins look at me expectantly. 'I suppose you have to go back now,' Pip says, and there's something wistful in her tiny voice. I realize that being found means leaving this strange, wonderful parliament behind. 'I suppose I do,' I say, but I'm already planning to ask if we can come back for another field trip. Some discoveries are too good to keep to yourself.

illustration.description: A chubby baby emperor penguin with fluffy grey-white down, round shiny black eyes, a tiny yellow beak, and oversized webbed feet stands among his new pumpkin friends as a teacher's silhouette appears in the distance, with the last golden light of day creating long purple shadows across the patch
illustration.alt: Waddles saying goodbye to his pumpkin friends as his teacher approaches

pages.text: Pip shows me the quickest way to Mrs. Featherworth's voice, through paths I never would have found alone. 'Come back tomorrow,' she whispers as I waddle away. 'It's Halloween, and we have special plans.' I turn to wave with my flipper, but she's already blending back into the patch, just another small pumpkin among hundreds. Mrs. Featherworth hugs me so hard my down gets ruffled, asking if I was scared. I think about my afternoon with the parliament and grin. 'Not even a little,' I tell her, and for once, that's completely true.

illustration.description: A chubby baby emperor penguin with fluffy grey-white down, round shiny black eyes, a tiny yellow beak, and oversized webbed feet gets a big hug from a concerned teacher while looking back over her shoulder at tiny Pip waving from among the pumpkins, with twilight purple light settling over the patch
illustration.alt: Waddles reunited with his teacher while saying goodbye to Pip

metadata.moralLesson: Sometimes getting lost leads to the best discoveries, and friendship can bloom in the most unexpected places.

coverPrompt.description: A chubby baby emperor penguin with fluffy grey-white down, round shiny black eyes, a tiny yellow beak, and oversized webbed feet stands among towering orange pumpkins at golden hour, with warm amber light filtering through autumn air and casting long purple shadows across the patch
coverPrompt.alt: Waddles the penguin in a glowing pumpkin patch at sunset

questions.question: Why does Waddles get left behind at the pumpkin patch?
questions.explanation: Mrs. Featherworth was counting heads but didn't count Waddles, so the bus left without him.

questions.question: Who is the first friend Waddles makes in the pumpkin patch?
questions.explanation: Pip is a tiny pumpkin who becomes Waddles' guide and first real friend in the patch.

questions.question: What do the pumpkins do during their evening meetings?
questions.explanation: The pumpkins hold discussions about the humans who visit them and take notes on their observations.

questions.question: How does Waddles feel when Mrs. Featherworth asks if he was scared?
questions.explanation: Waddles tells Mrs. Featherworth he wasn't scared 'not even a little' and means it completely.

questions.question: What special day is tomorrow in the story?
questions.explanation: Pip mentions that tomorrow is Halloween and the pumpkins have special plans.
